lines.en_lit: out of order and he was answered in order. And once he realized that he had asked out of order he went back and asked in order, as it is stated “Will the men of Keilah deliver me and my men into the hand of Saul? And God said: They will deliver you”

But if the matter requires two he is answered two as it is stated: “And David asked of God, saying: Shall I pursue after this troop? Will I overtake them? And He answered him: Pursue, for you will surely overtake them, and will surely rescue”
Even though a decree of a prophet can be retracted, a decree of the Urim VeTummim cannot be retracted. As it is stated: “By the judgment of the Urim”
Why is it called Urim VeTummim? Urim, it illuminates its words. Tummim, it fulfills its words,
And if you say: In the Gibeah of Benjamin why did not fulfill

they did not check whether they would be victorious or be defeated But on the last time, when they did check agreed as it is stated: “And Pinehas, the son of Elazar, the son of Aaron, stood before it in those days, saying: Shall I yet again go out to battle against the children of Benjamin my brother, or shall I cease? And God said: Go up, for tomorrow I will deliver him into your hand”
How is it done? Rabbi Yoḥanan says: protrude, Reish Lakish says: join
But tzadi is not written Rav Shmuel bar Yitzḥak said: Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob were written there. But surely tet was not written Rav Aḥa bar Ya’akov said: Shivtei Yeshurun, was written there.

raises an objection Any priest who does not speak with Divine Spirit and upon whom the Divine Presence does not rest is not consulted As Zadok inquired and it was effective for him, Ebiathar and it was not effective for him, As it is stated: “But Ebiathar went up until all the people had finished”

And may be consulted only on behalf of the king, From where are these matters Rabbi Abbahu said the verse states: “And he shall stand before Elazar the priest, who shall inquire for him by the judgment of the Urim “He”; this is to a king, “All the children of Israel with him”; this is the priest anointed for war, “Even all the congregation”; this is the Sanhedrin,

MISHNA: Yom Kippur, prohibited in eating and in drinking, and in bathing, and in smearing and in wearing shoes, and in conjugal relations. the king, bride wash their faces A woman after childbirth, wear shoes the statement of Rabbi Eliezer. The Rabbis prohibit
One who eats a large date-bulk and its pit, or who drinks a cheekful liable All foods join a date-bulk; and all liquids join a cheekful. one eats and drinks, do not join
GEMARA: prohibited, It punishable by karet Rabbi Ila said, and some say Rabbi Yirmeya said: needed only for a half-measure,
works out well according to one who said a half-measure is prohibited by Torah But according to one who says a half-measure is permitted by Torah what is to say

as it was stated a half-measure Rabbi Yoḥanan said: prohibited by Torah Reish Lakish said: permitted by Torah This well to Rabbi Yoḥanan. However, Reish Lakish, what can be said? Reish Lakish concedes that prohibited rabbinic
If so, one should not be liable to bring an offering an oath Why, learn an oath that I will not eat, and ate animal carcasses, tereifot, reptiles, or creeping animals, liable Rabbi Shimon exempts
And we discussed it: Why be liable He was already sworn and obligated at Mount Sinai, Rav, and Shmuel, and Rabbi Yoḥanan say includes permitted foods with forbidden foods. animal carcasses
And Reish Lakish said: you will find is explicitly a half-measure, and in accordance with the Rabbis. no specification
